item.title: GitHub Storage — Auth and Repo Browser
item.intent: GitHub authentication must be seamless (one-time OAuth) and repo browsing must feel as natural as the system file picker
item.description: Phase 2. GitHub OAuth device flow: user enters a code at github.com/login/device — no web view required in the app. Token stored in Keychain (not UserDefaults). Token refresh handled automatically. Sign out available in Settings. Repo browser: list user's repos (personal + orgs), search/filter, show last updated date. Navigate into repo to browse .md files. Repo list cached for offline access to previously cloned repos.
acceptanceCriteria: OAuth device flow completes without a web view embedded in the app
Token persists in Keychain across app launches
Repo browser shows personal and org repos
User can search/filter repos by name
Sign out in Settings clears token and offers to delete local clones
Expired token triggers re-auth flow automatically
